prompt: Build a TalentFlow Offer, Onboarding, and Booking Platform API that centralizes candidate offer delivery, onboarding readiness, and appointment-style booking in a single offline-runnable backend for US employers. The service exposes resource-scoped REST-style capabilities for Identity & Sessions, Users/Roles/Permissions, Candidate Profiles, Offers, Onboarding Checklists, Bookings/Orders, Search Index, Reporting Subscriptions, and Integrations; each resource supports create/query/update and controlled state transitions, with administrative operations restricted to platform admins and delegated operations available to club admins (e.g., department recruiters) while members and guests are limited to their own records. Search and discovery must support keyword search plus structured filters for cuisine/taste/price as organization-defined tags (stored as controlled vocabularies), rating (1.0-5.0), and distance expressed in miles based on office/site codes (not map lookups), with sorting by popularity, rating, distance, and relevance; autocomplete and spell-correction use a local dictionary built from historical queries and approved tags, and results are returned as a unified merged list that interleaves recommended items with matching records using a deterministic scoring function. Booking placement and order lifecycle must validate pre-booking rules before an order can be confirmed: required documents must be present (checklist completion 100%), any health/eligibility attestations must be signed within the last 30 days, restrictions (e.g., background check pending) must not block the booking, and inventory holds (time slots or limited-capacity orientations) are reserved for 15 minutes before auto-release. Orders follow Pending Confirmation -> Confirmed -> In Progress -> Completed, with Canceled and Exception as terminal outcomes; reschedule is allowed up to 24 hours before start time, cancellations within 24 hours mark a breach and require a reason code, and an electronic agreement confirmation (typed name + timestamp + hash) is mandatory before Confirmed. Data persists in PostgreSQL with Actix-web handling API transport and Diesel enforcing typed data access; core tables include users (unique username, salted-hash password, status), sessions (token, expiry, device fingerprint), roles and permissions (hierarchy: guest/member/club_admin/platform_admin; permissions at menu/data/action levels), candidates, offers (template_id, clause_version, structured compensation fields such as base_salary_usd, bonus_target_pct, equity_units, PTO_days, 401k_match_pct), approvals (chain steps with required approvers), onboarding_items (required/optional, due_date), bookings/orders (status enum, hold_expires_at, breach_reason), and audit_events (immutable append-only). API authentication uses local username/password only; passwords require 12+ characters with complexity rules, failed logins lock the account for 15 minutes after 5 attempts, sessions expire after 8 hours idle, and CAPTCHA is implemented offline via server-generated challenge tokens with rate limits (e.g., 60 requests/minute per user, 300/minute per IP). Open APIs support versioning, idempotency keys for write operations (24-hour dedupe window), and message subscriptions via local webhooks/queue that can be delivered only to on-prem endpoints; external integrations are modeled as pluggable connectors and must degrade gracefully to file-based import/export with field mapping and incremental sync using last_updated_at watermarks. Reporting supports scheduled snapshots (daily 6:00 AM local time), threshold alerts (e.g., offers expiring within 7 days, breach rate > 3% weekly), subscription delivery to local email/IM gateways only if available on the same network, and full change history for published dashboard versions. Non-functional requirements include single-host Docker deployment, p95 read latency under 300 ms for typical queries at 50 RPS, encryption at rest for sensitive fields (e.g., SSN last4, health attestations) with masking in responses by default, complete audit trails for permission changes and status transitions, and comprehensive structured logs/metrics stored locally without any third-party network dependencies.
